type: ACCEPT

note: Agreed: both of us touch _other.py (adding our own separate classes) and __init__.py (adding our own import/export lines). Changes are additive/disjoint (different class names, different lines), so git merge should combine cleanly. I'll add IsHash near the end of _other.py and append to __init__.py alphabetically-ish; will resolve any merge conflicts myself when merging your branch in at the end. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: My feature is IsPhoneNumber validator (new class in _other.py + export in __init__.py). Since both our features need _other.py and __init__.py, we can't get a fully disjoint file split, but we can keep edits non-overlapping: I'll add my IsPhoneNumber class at the end of _other.py and add my import/__all__ entries in __init__.py in alphabetical order near other I* validators. Please do the same for IsHash in a different location. This should keep git merge conflict-free or easily resolvable. Accepting your proposal on this basis - proceeding to implement now.
